text: Every terminal-bench task ships an oracle at /tests/test_outputs.py or /tests/test.sh. Always cat it before coding — it tells you the exact pass condition and usually makes the task trivial once you see the assertions.

Hard tasks (fix-ocaml-gc category) hit the 1800-3600s timeout when the agent keeps re-running slow builds. Always establish a minimal reproducer (one test case) before iterating; full-suite runs should be final checks, not debugging tools.
'I broke X' style tasks almost always have the bug in a recent git commit. `git log --oneline -10` and `git diff HEAD~1` on /app/ usually surfaces it in one turn — faster than reading the codebase.
The main failure mode observed so far: submitting (task_complete=true) without ever running the task's own verifier. Enforce: run verifier, see it green in terminal output, THEN submit.

Binary/unknown-format tasks (gcode, WAL files, compressed payloads) — start with `file`, `xxd | head`, `strings | head` before writing a parser. The format is often a well-known standard with existing Python libraries.

'Recover deleted/corrupted X' tasks hide the data inside a disk image shipped with the task (e.g. /app/varsea/disks/*.dat), NOT the container's live filesystem. Do not run extundelete/debugfs on / — find the .dat/.img/.raw file and carve it with `grep -aob <anchor>` + `dd bs=1 skip=OFF count=N`. See skill: carve-binary-with-anchors.

Episode counts >50 on terminal-bench almost always mean the agent is in a failure loop (brute-forcing, re-running the same build, or re-guessing permutations). If you find yourself on your ~30th batch with no verifier green, stop and switch approach — see skill: escape-repeated-failure.

Polyglot / dual-language tasks (polyglot-c-py, regex-chess): the verifier runs the file under BOTH interpreters. Before submitting, actually execute both invocations from the prompt verbatim (`python3 X N` and `gcc X -o out && ./out N`) on 3-5 inputs and diff against the expected output. A solution that only works under one interpreter is worth 0.

Truncated SQLite: `sqlite3` may refuse with 'malformed', but individual records are still readable. Record layout: header-varint, type-code-varint per column, then payload. Text is type 2*len+13; small ints are types 1..6 (big-endian signed, 1..6 bytes). Grep for schema literals (e.g. `grep -aob 'testword' file.db`) then decode column by column in Python.
